track_description: Background controls, maintenance, insulation, and sequencing improvements on incumbent medium-temperature fossil heat systems.
evidence_summary: Australian process-heat guidance supports a small but persistent background improvement in medium-temperature fossil heat systems once the same drift is made explicit instead of hidden in base rows.
derivation_method: Raise the applicable fossil state rows by the inverse of the accepted background multipliers and carry the intended drift explicitly through the autonomous track so the baseline pathway remains unchanged when the track is enabled.
double_counting_guardrail: Applies only to the fossil incumbent. Electrified and low-carbon-fuel route states remain excluded, and the base row is rebaselined so the track is not additive on top of the legacy trajectory.
review_notes: The effect stays deliberately modest and fossil-only until finer industrial process splits support a richer treatment.
